verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46) is a single-pass type-I integral membrane protein predominantly resident in the trans-Golgi network (TGN), where its TM domain and cytoplasmic tail contain retention/retrieval signals. However, it is well-established that TGN46 cycles continuously between the TGN and the plasma membrane via endosomes, transiently appearing on the cell surface with its own TM anchor intact before being retrieved via the endocytic pathway. This TGN→PM→endosome→TGN trafficking cycle constitutes a documented minority PM pool, qualifying as dual_localization. The dominant compartment is the TGN (endomembrane), but the protein body does reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane via its own TM anchor during this cycling. No evidence of cancer-specific, cell-death-induced, activation-induced, or germline-restricted ectopic surface display was identified beyond the constitutive cycling.
